page: Amazon Web Services m6g.8xlarge is a general-purpose server instance powered by custom AWS Graviton processors. It features 32 dedicated physical cores based on the 64-bit ARMv8 architecture (Neoverse-N1 model) running at 2.5 GHz. The system provides 128.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced ratio of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. Built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, the instance allocates dedicated CPU resources with one thread per core, ensuring no resource sharing at the hardware thread level. It lacks local storage and dedicated GPU hardware, relying entirely on network-attached storage and offering a baseline network bandwidth of 12 Gbps.

Benchmark results place the m6g.8xlarge in the average tier for most multi-core and memory operations, though single-core performance shows some limitations. The stress-ng multi-core CPU test scored 48570.2, and the Geekbench multi-core score reached 11654.0, both falling within the average range. However, single-core metrics such as the Geekbench single-core score of 955.0 and single-threaded gzip compression at 20.36 GB/sec are categorized as weak. Memory bandwidth is robust for cached reads at 1893.6 GB/sec, but drops to 178.7 GB/sec for uncached large blocks, while the overall PassMark memory score is relatively weak.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ARM64 architecture provides a resource-dense alternative to traditional x86 platforms, delivering balanced compute and memory capacities without the overhead of hyperthreading. The instance demonstrates solid capabilities for database operations, with PassMark database operations scoring 20309.54 and Redis SET operations reaching 36.99 million operations per second. It is also capable of handling static web serving at 32.53 GB/sec and lightweight machine learning tasks, such as text generation on a small 135M model at 76.77 tokens per second. This profile makes the instance suitable for multi-threaded workloads, web serving, and database applications that benefit from dedicated physical cores.
description: Amazon Web Services m6g.8xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 32 dedicated physical cores powered by the 64-bit ARMv8 AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 processor running at 2.5 GHz. It is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core, and operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor. The instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, and it features a baseline network bandwidth of 12 Gbps. Multi-core performance is solid, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 11654.0 and a PassMark CPU score of 17382.94, though single-core metrics and memory performance benchmarks are relatively weak. This hardware profile offers a cost-efficient, resource-dense option for multi-threaded workloads, database operations, static web serving, and lightweight machine learning inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m6g.8xlarge is an ARM64 general-purpose instance featuring 32 dedicated Graviton physical cores, 128 GB of RAM, and a 12 Gbps network baseline, delivering balanced multi-core performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m6g.8xlarge is an ARM64 general-purpose instance with 32 dedicated Graviton cores, 128 GB RAM, and solid multi-core performance.
tagline: A balanced ARM64 general-purpose platform featuring thirty-two dedicated physical cores and one hundred twenty-eight gigabytes of memory for multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by 32 dedicated AWS Graviton ARMv8 physical cores running at 2.5 GHz
Equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory providing a balanced 4.0 GB per core
Delivers 12 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage or GPU
Demonstrates solid multi-core performance in database and web serving benchmarks
Suited for multi-threaded applications, database operations, and static web serving
